question:
Will MrBeast's trap video get between 50 and 55 million views on week 1?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of views the trap video posted by MrBeast gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's "100 People Vs World's Biggest Trap!" video. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

evidences.content: en English Deutsch عربي Português Español 日本語 中文 Bahasa Indonesia Polski 한국어 My Account Sign Out Guinness World Records # Most viewed challenge YouTube video in 24 hours Most viewed challenge YouTube video in 24 hours Who : Jimmy Donaldson aka 'MrBeast' What : 80,995,076 view(s) Where : United States When : 14 July 2023 The most viewed challenge YouTube video in 24 hours is '50 YouTubers Fight For $1,000,000' with 80,995,076 views and was achieved by Jimmy Donaldson aka 'MrBeast' (USA) as of 14 July 2024. The video included appearances from the likes of Logan Paul, KSI and IShowSpeed. Tyler Conklin and Sean Klitzner were involved in helping make this achievement record-breaking.

evidences.title: MrBeast
evidences.content: On June 2, 2024, Donaldson surpassed India-based music label and film production company T-Series "T-Series (company)") for the title of the most subscribed channel on YouTube, with 267 million. On July 10, 2024, Donaldson became the first YouTuber to surpass 300 million subscribers. On July 13, 2024, Donaldson uploaded his 300 million subscribers special, "50 YouTubers Fight for $1,000,000". The video featured guest appearances from Howie Mandel, Miranda Cosgrove, and Joey Chestnut. This video reached 71 million views in the first 24 hours, becoming Donaldson's most-viewed video within its first 24 hours. In his video "Beat Ronaldo, Win $1,000,000", Cristiano Ronaldo, Tom Brady, and Bryson DeChambeau made guest appearances. Archived from the original on July 18, 2024. Retrieved July 19, 2024. 82. ↑ @Mr...

evidences.title: Introducing ViewStats: A data platform for YouTube creators co-founded by MrBeast (Exclusive) - Tubefilter
evidences.content: Tubefilter Brand Reports Charts Creators YouTube TikTok Newsletter # Introducing ViewStats: A data platform for YouTube creators co-founded by MrBeast (Exclusive) By Sam Gutelle • 12/13/2023 • Introducing ViewStats: A data platform for YouTube creators co-founded by MrBeast (Exclusive) Jimmy Donaldson has become the most-subscribed creator on YouTube by taking an analytical approach to his big-budget videos. The man known as MrBeast is famous for his detail-oriented personality and his ability to maximize every second of his viewer’s attention. [...] As he continued to analyze videos alongside Donaldson, Appleby looked to share his findings with the YouTube masses. “We realized that we should create a data site to help people understand their analytics, to make it easier to make better video ideas,” Appleby told Tubefilter. “[Jimmy and I] are obsessed with content. And our approach was to say, ‘We are only going to add features that we know actually affect the performance of a YouTube video.'” [...] ### Subscribe to get the latest creator news Subscribe ViewStats is a database that lets users apply MrBeast-style analysis to their videos. The metrics available on the site include...

evidences.title: MrBeast is launching a "Pro" version of his analytics platform ViewStats - Tubefilter
evidences.content: 1. Outliers, which attempts to explain why some videos get exponentially more views than others. 2. Thumbnail Search, which finds thumbnails that are similar to the ones selected by users. 3. Access to “every A/B thumbnail test ran on YouTube,” according to Donaldson. Thumbnail testing is a key component of the ViewStats experience. 4. Alertsthat generate video ideas by letting creators know when other channels upload videos that touch on the topics they’re interested in. 5. Collectionsthat group together multiple ViewStats Pro videos in one space. Donaldson and Youshaei applied Outliers analysis to MrBeast videos during their conversation: [...] Tubefilter Brand Reports Charts Creators YouTube TikTok Newsletter # MrBeast is launching a “Pro” version of his analytics platform ViewStats By Sam Gutelle • 05/29/2024 • MrBeast is launching a “Pro” version of his analytics platform ViewStats ViewStatsis going pro. evidences.content: Deep Video Analytics is TubeAnalytics' video-level reporting tool that tracks view velocity, engagement heatmaps, and performance forecasting for every video on your channel. evidences.title: Here are the influencer marketing trends that could define YouTube in 2024 - Tubefilter
evidences.content: MrBeastis the most popular creator on YouTube. Digital learning platforms like to partner with educational channels like Veritasium and Kurzgesagt. VPN companies like to partner with…pretty much everyone. We also drew some insights that felt new and noteworthy. Influencer marketing may not be as novel as it once was, but the industry is still subject to constant change, especially after experiencing a boom during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic. Tubefilter ### Subscribe for daily Tubefilter Top Stories Subscribe In all our analysis of 2023 YouTuber campaigns, four recurring themes stood out. As 2024 is underway, look out for these trends (in no particular order): ### 1. The sponsor pool is diversifying in surprising ways. [...] Creators are well-equipped to advertise their own products, since they can have perfect understanding of their campaign goals and direct access to their target audience (i.e. their subscribers). That’s why Crunchlabs is featured in most Mark Rober videos, Prime shows up in most of Logan Paul‘s Impaulsiveepisodes, and Feastables is a regular MrBeast sponsor. Jimmy Donaldsonlikes to shill on behalf of his friends, too. He took Prime to the top of the Go...

evidences.title: MrBeast on His Swift Rise on YouTube and Beyond
evidences.content: Donaldson, at a gaming event in July 2022, is an avid gamer and anime fan. Donaldson, at a gaming event in July 2022, is an avid gamer and anime fan. Denise Truscello—Getty Images for Amazon's Crown Channel Despite his youth and prominence, Donaldson has largely managed to stay out of the cultural crosshairs. In 2018, the Atlantic dug up videos of him using a homo­phobic slur, which were largely shrugged off as adolescent ignorance. “I did not even know what [it] meant,” he says, spelling out the word. Some participants in his videos have complained that it was a grueling experience. And as his staff has swelled—he now has 300 employees on his production team plus 200 or so more at Feastables—there have been growing pains. [...] Donaldson acknowledges that these sting, but persists. “I like to do those kinds of videos, because it gives younger kids someone to look up to and be like, ‘Oh, that’s cool,’” he says. “And instead of drinking alcohol, or doing drugs, it’s a role model that they can be like, ‘He does good and helps people.’” Donaldson defends his philanthropic work against charges of poverty profiteering by pointing out that it is funded by his main channel, which sends...
